documents.title: Phishing
documents.content:
Attack: Phishing
Summary: Fraudulent messages that trick users into revealing credentials or opening malicious links or files.
Detection signals: Unexpected credential prompts, urgent language, spoofed domains, mismatched links, attachment pressure.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Spear Phishing
documents.content:
Attack: Spear Phishing
Summary: Targeted phishing tailored to a specific person, team, or organization.
Detection signals: Context-rich lure, personalized content, impersonation of internal staff or vendors.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Business Email Compromise
documents.content:
Attack: Business Email Compromise
Summary: Social engineering aimed at fraudulent payments or sensitive business actions.
Detection signals: Executive impersonation, invoice changes, payment urgency, mailbox-forwarding anomalies.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Credential Stuffing
documents.content:
Attack: Credential Stuffing
Summary: Automated login attempts using reused username/password pairs from previous breaches.
Detection signals: High login volume across many accounts from few IPs, rapid failures followed by sparse successes.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Password Spraying
documents.content:
Attack: Password Spraying
Summary: Trying a small set of common passwords across many accounts to avoid lockouts.
Detection signals: One password against many users, low-and-slow failures, broad account distribution.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Brute Force
documents.content:
Attack: Brute Force
Summary: Repeated guessing of credentials for one or more accounts.
Detection signals: Repeated failures on same account or from same source, increasing velocity, lockout triggers.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: MFA Fatigue
documents.content:
Attack: MFA Fatigue
Summary: Repeated MFA push prompts intended to coerce user approval.
Detection signals: Burst of push prompts, odd hours, user-approved after many denials.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Token Theft
documents.content:
Attack: Token Theft
Summary: Stealing session or refresh tokens to bypass password/MFA checks.
Detection signals: Impossible travel with same session, refresh anomalies, sign-ins without password events.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: SQL Injection
documents.content:
Attack: SQL Injection
Summary: Injecting SQL syntax into database queries constructed from untrusted input.
Detection signals: Unexpected SQL errors, quote-based payloads, UNION/OR 1=1 patterns, auth bypass attempts.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Cross-Site Scripting
documents.content:
Attack: Cross-Site Scripting
Summary: Injecting script into web content viewed by other users.
Detection signals: Script tags or event handlers in parameters, stored content triggering outbound requests.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Cross-Site Request Forgery
documents.content:
Attack: Cross-Site Request Forgery
Summary: Abusing an authenticated browser to perform unintended actions.
Detection signals: State-changing requests without CSRF token or origin checks.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Command Injection
documents.content:
Attack: Command Injection
Summary: Injecting OS shell syntax into server-side command execution.
Detection signals: Unexpected shell metacharacters, new process trees, suspicious child processes.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Path Traversal
documents.content:
Attack: Path Traversal
Summary: Manipulating file paths to access unintended files.
Detection signals: Requests containing ../, encoded traversal patterns, sensitive file reads.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Server-Side Request Forgery
documents.content:
Attack: Server-Side Request Forgery
Summary: Forcing a server to make requests to unintended internal or external resources.
Detection signals: Outbound requests to metadata endpoints, internal IPs, odd URL schemes.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: XML External Entity
documents.content:
Attack: XML External Entity
Summary: Abusing unsafe XML parsing to read files or reach internal services.
Detection signals: DOCTYPE/entity payloads, parser errors around external entities, unexpected outbound connections.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Insecure Deserialization
documents.content:
Attack: Insecure Deserialization
Summary: Unsafe object deserialization leading to code execution or logic abuse.
Detection signals: Serialized object markers in requests, gadget-chain crashes, unsafe deserializer use.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Remote Code Execution
documents.content:
Attack: Remote Code Execution
Summary: Running attacker-controlled code on a target system.
Detection signals: Unexpected interpreter processes, web app spawning shells, new services or files.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Privilege Escalation
documents.content:
Attack: Privilege Escalation
Summary: Gaining higher privileges than originally granted.
Detection signals: Sudo misuse, token abuse, new admin group membership, protected resource access.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Web Shell
documents.content:
Attack: Web Shell
Summary: A server-side script that gives attackers remote command capability.
Detection signals: Tiny newly uploaded scripts, rare POST activity, command execution signatures, odd child processes.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Malware
documents.content:
Attack: Malware
Summary: Malicious software used for theft, disruption, or persistence.
Detection signals: Persistence changes, suspicious startup entries, outbound C2 traffic, tamper attempts.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Ransomware
documents.content:
Attack: Ransomware
Summary: Malware that encrypts or locks data for extortion.
Detection signals: Mass file renames, shadow copy deletion, encryption bursts, ransom note artifacts.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Spyware
documents.content:
Attack: Spyware
Summary: Software that covertly collects sensitive information.
Detection signals: Keylogging behavior, screen capture, unauthorized data exfiltration.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Trojan
documents.content:
Attack: Trojan
Summary: Malware disguised as legitimate software or files.
Detection signals: Execution after lure download, masquerading names, unexpected network beacons.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Worm
documents.content:
Attack: Worm
Summary: Self-propagating malware that spreads across systems.
Detection signals: Rapid lateral spread, repeated exploitation traffic, simultaneous host infections.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Botnet Activity
documents.content:
Attack: Botnet Activity
Summary: Compromised hosts controlled as a group for attacks or abuse.
Detection signals: Periodic beaconing, synchronized traffic, known C2 indicators.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Distributed Denial of Service
documents.content:
Attack: Distributed Denial of Service
Summary: Overwhelming a service with traffic or requests.
Detection signals: Traffic spikes, protocol abuse, resource exhaustion, service degradation.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Man-in-the-Middle
documents.content:
Attack: Man-in-the-Middle
Summary: Intercepting or modifying traffic between parties.
Detection signals: TLS downgrade signs, certificate mismatches, ARP anomalies, proxy insertion.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: DNS Spoofing
documents.content:
Attack: DNS Spoofing
Summary: Returning forged DNS answers to redirect traffic.
Detection signals: Unexpected DNS responses, resolver inconsistency, suspicious TTL patterns.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: ARP Spoofing
documents.content:
Attack: ARP Spoofing
Summary: Poisoning local network ARP caches to intercept traffic.
Detection signals: MAC changes for same gateway IP, duplicate ARP replies, packet path anomalies.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: DNS Tunneling
documents.content:
Attack: DNS Tunneling
Summary: Using DNS queries/responses to move data or C2 traffic.
Detection signals: Long random subdomains, high TXT query volume, unusual entropy, beacon cadence.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Beaconing
documents.content:
Attack: Beaconing
Summary: Regular outbound check-ins to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
Detection signals: Low-and-slow periodic traffic, uniform intervals, repeated domains or IPs.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Data Exfiltration
documents.content:
Attack: Data Exfiltration
Summary: Unauthorized transfer of sensitive data out of the environment.
Detection signals: Large outbound transfers, rare destinations, compressed/encrypted archives, staging files.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Supply Chain Attack
documents.content:
Attack: Supply Chain Attack
Summary: Compromise through dependencies, vendors, or build pipelines.
Detection signals: Unexpected package updates, signature mismatches, build artifact drift.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Insider Threat
documents.content:
Attack: Insider Threat
Summary: Malicious or negligent activity by a trusted internal user.
Detection signals: Access outside role, unusual downloads, after-hours actions, policy bypass.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Cloud Misconfiguration Abuse
documents.content:
Attack: Cloud Misconfiguration Abuse
Summary: Exposure or compromise caused by insecure cloud settings.
Detection signals: Public buckets, overly broad IAM, disabled logging, open admin endpoints.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: API Abuse
documents.content:
Attack: API Abuse
Summary: Misuse of APIs for scraping, privilege bypass, or resource exhaustion.
Detection signals: High request burst, missing auth checks, object enumeration, quota evasion.
Defensive response: validate logs, tighten authentication or access controls, contain affected systems, preserve evidence, and monitor for recurrence.

documents.title: Detecting Phishing
documents.content: Look for urgent language, spoofed sender domains, credential prompts, payment pressure, unexpected attachments, and displayed links that differ from actual destinations.

documents.title: Detecting Credential Stuffing
documents.content: Correlate high authentication failure rates across many accounts from limited source IPs, then look for a small number of successes using the same infrastructure.

documents.title: Detecting Password Spraying
documents.content: Alert when the same password pattern or same source hits many usernames with low failure count per user over an extended window.

documents.title: Detecting Brute Force
documents.content: Track repeated authentication failures on the same account, especially when followed by account lockout, MFA prompts, or eventual success.

documents.title: Detecting MFA Fatigue
documents.content: Flag many denied MFA prompts followed by a single approval, especially from new devices or foreign geographies.

documents.title: Detecting SQL Injection
documents.content: Inspect logs and WAF events for quote-heavy payloads, UNION/SELECT markers, comment operators, OR 1=1 patterns, and sudden SQL parser errors.

documents.title: Detecting XSS
documents.content: Monitor for script tags, event handlers, javascript: URIs, reflected parameters, and outbound browser requests triggered by viewed content.

documents.title: Detecting SSRF
documents.content: Alert on server-side outbound requests to metadata endpoints, localhost-like resources, internal ranges, and uncommon schemes requested by user-controlled parameters.

documents.title: Detecting Command Injection
documents.content: Correlate user-supplied input containing shell metacharacters with new child processes, shell invocation, or abnormal process lineage.

documents.title: Detecting Path Traversal
documents.content: Search request logs for ../, encoded traversal strings, access to config or passwd-like files, and repeated 403/404 patterns near file endpoints.

documents.title: Detecting Remote Code Execution
documents.content: Alert on web-facing services spawning shells, interpreters, compilers, or network tools unexpectedly, especially after crafted HTTP requests.

documents.title: Detecting Web Shells
documents.content: Watch for newly written executable scripts in web roots, rare POSTs to small files, and web servers creating child shell processes.

documents.title: Detecting Ransomware
documents.content: Detect mass file modifications, extension changes, shadow copy deletion, high entropy writes, and disabling of security controls.

documents.title: Detecting Malware Beaconing
documents.content: Look for periodic outbound connections with regular intervals, low data volumes, and repeated destinations or JA3-like fingerprints.

documents.title: Detecting DNS Tunneling
documents.content: Identify very long or random-looking subdomains, unusual TXT activity, persistent NXDOMAIN bursts, and high-entropy labels.

documents.title: Detecting DDoS
documents.content: Measure abnormal traffic spikes, protocol-specific floods, elevated error rates, and saturation of CPU, bandwidth, or connection tables.

documents.title: Detecting Data Exfiltration
documents.content: Flag large outbound transfers, compressed archive creation, movement to rare destinations, and off-hours downloads from sensitive sources.

documents.title: Detecting Insider Threat
documents.content: Correlate unusual data access, broad searches, privilege misuse, removable media use, and after-hours activity by trusted users.

documents.title: Detecting Cloud Abuse
documents.content: Audit for public exposure, IAM drift, disabled logging, new access keys, role assumptions, and suspicious API call bursts.

documents.title: Detecting Token Theft
documents.content: Look for session reuse from impossible travel locations, browser fingerprint drift, refresh anomalies, and access without normal password events.

documents.title: Common Cyber Attack Categories
documents.content: Phishing, spear phishing, business email compromise, credential stuffing, password spraying, brute force, MFA fatigue, token theft, SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, command injection, path traversal, SSRF, XXE, insecure deserialization, RCE, privilege escalation, web shells, malware, ransomware, spyware, trojans, worms, botnets, DDoS, man-in-the-middle, DNS spoofing, ARP spoofing, DNS tunneling, beaconing, data exfiltration, supply chain attacks, insider threats, cloud misconfiguration abuse, and API abuse.
